title: Accessible static site + printable PDF handouts + structured-data export (no accounts, no PII, no tracking)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task delivers the corpus as an accessible, offline-friendly, low-bandwidth static site plus print-ready PDF handouts and a structured-data export - with no accounts, no PII fields, and no profiling/tracking analytics, confirmed by privacy review.
objective: Build the accessible static site, print-ready PDF handouts, and structured-data export, with zero PII and no tracking, confirmed by privacy review.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders the corpus as an accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA), offline-friendly, low-bandwidth site; generates print-ready plain-language PDF handouts; exports the structured data for reuse
No accounts, no PII fields, no profiling/tracking analytics; privacy review confirms zero-PII/trackless delivery
The fixed not-advice framing and route-to-human pointers are present on every rendered guide and handout
